method_atom: An exactly solvable model of coupled two-state units on a network with bond-direction-dependent pairwise couplings, diagonalized by mapping it onto non-interacting fermionic modes coupled to a static Z2 gauge field, yielding a parameter-space phase diagram with gapped and gapless regimes. The gapped free-fermion spectra are classified by a topological invariant (a spectral Chern number); this invariant taken modulo 16 fixes the fractional braiding statistics of localized topological excitations, while the invariant itself governs quantized edge (boundary) transport, with an accompanying elementary theory of the Chern number for quasidiagonal matrices.
object_atom: A spin-1/2 system on a honeycomb lattice with XX, YY, or ZZ nearest-neighbor interactions selected by link direction, whose gapped and gapless phases host Abelian and (in a magnetic field) non-Abelian anyonic excitations whose braiding matches Ising-model conformal blocks and which carry edge thermal transport.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the free-fermion-plus-Z2-gauge-field exact solution and Chern-number classification of topological excitations and edge transport; the domain-specific part is the honeycomb spin-1/2 model and its anyon spectrum, and here method and object are strongly fused because the reduction is defined for this specific lattice interaction structure.
